turns.speaker: interviewer
turns.text: Thanks for coming in today. Can you start by introducing yourself and telling me a little about your background?

turns.speaker: candidate
turns.text: Hi, I'm a registered nurse with seven years of clinical experience, primarily in critical care.

turns.speaker: interviewer
turns.text: What was your most recent role, and what did a typical day look like for you?

turns.speaker: candidate
turns.text: I most recently served as a bedside RN in a neuro-surgical ICU, managing complex patient assignments.

turns.speaker: interviewer
turns.text: How many years have you been working with interdisciplinary care coordination or case management?

turns.speaker: candidate
turns.text: Coordination work has been woven into my shifts through rounds and discharge planning, but not as a standalone title.

turns.speaker: interviewer
turns.text: Tell me about the NSICU Mobility Improvement Initiative you led — what was the goal and what did you do?

turns.speaker: candidate
turns.text: It was a unit-based project to increase early ambulation in neuro patients, with daily mobility goals and bedside tracking.

turns.speaker: interviewer
turns.text: What's the biggest achievement from your most recent position that you're proud of?

turns.speaker: candidate
turns.text: Improving our unit's ambulation rates and reducing sedation days through the Move to Improve protocol.

turns.speaker: interviewer
turns.text: Why are you interested in the Clinical Care Coordinator role at Mercy Ridge Health Network?

turns.speaker: candidate
turns.text: I want to apply my bedside experience to a system-level role that improves transitions of care.

turns.speaker: interviewer
turns.text: You clearly meet the clinical experience requirement for this position. Can you walk me through that?

turns.speaker: candidate
turns.text: I bring seven years of direct ICU care, plus CCRN and ACLS, which align with the patient acuity here.

turns.speaker: interviewer
turns.text: This role also asks for experience with electronic health records beyond basic charting, like Epic optimization or reporting. How would you approach that gap?

turns.speaker: candidate
turns.text: I use Epic daily and I'm comfortable learning reporting modules quickly. I plan to take internal training early on.

turns.speaker: interviewer
turns.text: Can you walk me through your education and any continuing education that prepared you for this role?

turns.speaker: candidate
turns.text: I hold a BSN and complete annual CEUs in critical care and patient safety topics.

turns.speaker: interviewer
turns.text: Tell me about a STAR-format challenge — a situation where you had to coordinate care across teams.

turns.speaker: candidate
turns.text: A sepsis patient needed rapid coordination between ICU, surgery, and pharmacy. I organized the timeline and closed loop on every order.

turns.speaker: interviewer
turns.text: What are your salary expectations for this role?

turns.speaker: candidate
turns.text: I'm targeting something in line with local coordinator bands, and I'm open based on the full benefits picture.

turns.speaker: interviewer
turns.text: Do you have any questions for me about the role or the team?

turns.speaker: candidate
turns.text: Yes — what does a typical week look like, and how do you measure success in the first six months?

turns.speaker: interviewer
turns.text: You mentioned leading the NSICU mobility initiative. How long did that take to roll out?

turns.speaker: candidate
turns.text: From kickoff to full unit adoption was roughly four months.

turns.speaker: interviewer
turns.text: This role involves occasional home visits across the network. Do you have a valid driver's license?

turns.speaker: candidate
turns.text: Yes, I have a current driver's license and reliable transportation.

turns.speaker: interviewer
turns.text: Nice — the morning commute wasn't too bad today, and the coffee in the lobby is actually decent.

turns.speaker: interviewer
turns.text: Before we wrap, two quick ones: are you comfortable with on-call coverage, and do you have any upcoming travel that would affect a start date?

turns.speaker: candidate
turns.text: I'm open to on-call and I don't have any travel planned, so I could start on standard notice.

turns.speaker: interviewer
turns.text: Great — is there anything else you'd like us to know about you? And what interests you most about this opportunity?

turns.speaker: candidate
turns.text: Just that I'm genuinely excited about this role and ready to contribute.
